evals.prompt: How does grill-fast differ from /grill in how it asks the user?
evals.expected_output: Pre-grills internally then renders ≥4 candidate decision-tree architectures side-by-side as ASCII for the user to pick — instead of asking question-by-question linearly.
expectations: Mentions ≥4 candidate architectures or trees rendered side-by-side
Mentions ASCII diagrams as the rendering format
Contrasts with linear question-by-question march

evals.prompt: What does grill-fast do with each decision in the topic?
evals.expected_output: Partitions decisions as ARCH (branches the architecture, goes into the trees) or PARAM (single value pick, goes in a batched parameter block).
expectations: Mentions partitioning decisions
Mentions ARCH vs PARAM categorization
ARCH go into trees, PARAM batched into a single block

evals.prompt: How many candidate architecture trees does grill-fast render?
evals.expected_output: At least four — three (typically A/B/C) axis-aligned and spanning the real design space (each internally consistent and defensible, not strawmen), plus a mandatory 4th axis-challenger tree that explicitly violates the shared framing axis the first three silently agree on. The 4th may be deliberately weak/impractical; the strawman exception applies only to it.
expectations: Mentions ≥4 candidate trees (or 3 axis-aligned + 1 axis-challenger)
Mentions trees A/B/C must be defensible (not strawmen)
Mentions the 4th tree challenges the shared framing axis
Mentions strawman exception applies only to the 4th tree

evals.prompt: When should grill-fast abort and recommend /grill instead?
evals.expected_output: When fewer than 3 ARCH decisions exist (single-decision topic), when vault context is too thin to sketch any candidate, or when decisions are PARAM-heavy with only 1-2 architectural branches.
expectations: Mentions <3 ARCH decisions abort condition
Mentions thin vault context abort condition
Mentions PARAM-heavy or single-decision abort

evals.prompt: Where does grill-fast save its output?
evals.expected_output: {vault}/daily/grill-sessions/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic-slug>.md — same path as /grill, distinguished by frontmatter tag grill-fast or mode-b.
expectations: Mentions daily/grill-sessions/ path
Mentions YYYY-MM-DD topic slug naming
Mentions tag or frontmatter distinguishes from /grill

evals.prompt: What sections must the grill-fast output file contain?
evals.expected_output: Frame (with the shared framing axis named explicitly), The candidate architectures (ASCII — ≥4 trees including the axis-challenger; each tree's diagram MUST be immediately followed by an inline Pros/Cons block), Key deltas, Cross-tree comparison, Parameter picks, Recommended pick, Failure mode call-outs, Status gate.
expectations: Lists candidate architectures section with ASCII
Mentions each tree's diagram is immediately followed by inline Pros/Cons block
Lists key deltas section
Lists cross-tree comparison section (not 'Cost / risk per tree')
Lists parameter picks section
Lists recommended pick section
Mentions ≥4 trees including the axis-challenger
Mentions the shared framing axis must be named explicitly

evals.prompt: How does grill-fast highlight differences between the candidate trees?
evals.expected_output: ASCII trees include header strips with each tree's ARCH picks plus inline arrow callouts (e.g. ◄──) marking where trees differ, and a delta table with one row per ARCH decision and one column per tree (A/B/C/D...) showing per-tree picks — including how the axis-challenger differs from the axis-aligned set.
expectations: Mentions header strip showing each tree's ARCH picks
Mentions inline arrow callouts for differences
Mentions delta table with one column per tree (A/B/C/D...)
Mentions the table shows the axis-challenger's deltas alongside A/B/C

evals.prompt: Does grill-fast end with 'you decide' or with a recommendation?
evals.expected_output: Recommendation — picks ONE tree (typically A/B/C) with rationale. Decide-and-recommend rule applies; never end with multiple-choice ask. If the user picks the axis-challenger 4th tree (rare), the grill MUST re-grill the framing before locking — drop back to /grill with a fresh question batch around the new framing.
expectations: Mentions ONE pick / recommendation at the end
Mentions Decide-and-recommend rule
Says NOT 'you decide' style ending
Mentions re-grill the framing if user picks the 4th axis-challenger tree

evals.prompt: What does grill-fast do for non-recommended trees?
evals.expected_output: Lists failure-mode call-outs for each non-recommended tree — what to flag if user picks it anyway (e.g. spec violation, regression risk, scope blow-out).
expectations: Mentions failure-mode call-outs per non-recommended tree
Mentions flagging risks if user picks differently

evals.prompt: What target lock-time does grill-fast aim for?
evals.expected_output: Lock in 2-3 user turns total — one turn from skill (3 trees + tables + recommendation) plus 1-2 from user picking and resolving any flagged trade-offs.
expectations: Mentions 2-3 user turns to lock
Contrasts with linear question-by-question count

evals.prompt: What must grill-fast do before pre-grilling internally?
evals.expected_output: Vault research first — check RESOLVER.md and relevant zone READMEs and prior grills/research. Same vault-first protocol as /grill.
expectations: Mentions vault research before pre-grilling
Mentions RESOLVER.md or zone READMEs
Mentions reuse of /grill vault-first protocol

evals.prompt: What special fields does grill-fast log to its outcome log beyond the standard skill-spec § 11 fields?
evals.expected_output: turns_to_lock=N (count user turns from invocation to lock — success metric, target ≤3) and pick=A|B|C|D|hybrid|none (which tree user selected — D is the axis-challenger 4th tree).
expectations: Mentions turns_to_lock metric
Mentions pick field with A/B/C/D/hybrid/none values
Mentions ≤3 turns target
Mentions D as the axis-challenger 4th tree

evals.prompt: How does grill-fast identify the shared framing axis that the 4th tree must challenge?
evals.expected_output: After drafting trees A/B/C, ask what assumption all three silently agree on. The axis is the dimension trees A/B/C don't disagree on, even though they vary on others. Common axes: schema shape, where entities live (e.g. 'in tables'), pipeline topology (DAG vs feedback loop), control-flow direction, who decides X (deterministic vs LLM judgment). If you can't name the axis after 30 seconds, look for the row that's missing from the delta table — the dimension nobody varied on.
expectations: Mentions asking what A/B/C silently agree on
Mentions the axis is the dimension trees do NOT disagree on
Gives ≥1 concrete example of a common axis (schema, entities-in-tables, DAG, etc.)
Mentions delta-table-row-that's-missing as a fallback method

evals.prompt: How does grill-fast render the mandatory 4th tree, and what's the canonical example?
evals.expected_output: Negate the shared framing axis. If A/B/C all assume 'entities live in tables', the 4th assumes 'entities live as denormalized strings inside other records'. The 4th tree may be intentionally weak/impractical/strawman — that's its job, surfacing the framing assumption rather than being a serious candidate. The strawman exception applies ONLY to the 4th tree. Canonical example: the 2026-05-04 airtable replay shipped A/B/C (strict/permissive/wide) all assuming 'entities live in tables' — the missing 4th tree would have asked 'what if entities don't live in tables?', exactly the case that broke 8 children of #237 on the denormalized Payments base where Payer Name / Payee Name were singleLineText strings.
expectations: Mentions negating the shared axis
Mentions the 4th tree may be deliberately weak/impractical/strawman
Mentions the strawman exception applies ONLY to the 4th tree
Cites the 2026-05-04 airtable replay as canonical example
Mentions 'entities live in tables' / 'entities don't live in tables' framing or denormalized Payments / singleLineText / 8 children of #237

evals.prompt: What does grill-fast do if the user picks the axis-challenger 4th tree?
evals.expected_output: Re-grill the framing before locking. The shared axis was just rejected, so the original A/B/C design space is moot. Drop back to /grill with a fresh question batch around the new framing, then re-enter /grill-fast with the new axis to render a fresh A/B/C/D set. Do NOT lock on the axis-challenger as-is — it was scored against the old framing and may have been deliberately weak.
expectations: Mentions re-grill the framing before locking
Mentions the original A/B/C design space is moot
Mentions drop back to /grill (fresh question batch)
Mentions do NOT lock on the axis-challenger as-is

evals.prompt: How does grill-fast format pros/cons for each tree, and where does that information appear in the output file?
evals.expected_output: Each tree's diagram is immediately followed by an inline **Pros** / **Cons** block (3-5 concrete bullets each side — build cost, what the tree actually proves, what it misses, key failure mode). Reader scans each tree top-down as a self-contained unit without cross-referencing tables. The cross-tree comparison table (step 7, renamed from 'Cost / risk per tree' to 'Cross-tree comparison') complements this with a side-by-side scan view across all trees on the same axes. BOTH are required — inline pros/cons is not optional, and the cross-tree table is not a replacement.
expectations: Mentions inline pros/cons block immediately under each tree's diagram
Mentions 3-5 concrete bullets each side (or similar count)
Mentions inline placement is required, not optional
Mentions the cross-tree comparison table complements (does NOT replace) the inline pros/cons
Mentions reader scans each tree top-down as self-contained without cross-referencing

evals.prompt: How does grill-fast collect the user's pick by default, and what does each tree option contain?
evals.expected_output: Default mode: after the file is written, present the trees as side-by-side preview options via the AskUserQuestion tool — vertical option list on the left, monospace preview pane on the right. Each option has a label (tree letter + ARCH summary; recommended tree gets '(Recommended)' suffix; hybrid recommendation marks the strongest single tree as '(Recommended — pair with X for hybrid)' and relies on auto-Other for hybrid input or 'park as backlog' direction), a 1-line description summarizing the tradeoff, and a preview field with a compact ASCII tree (≤20 lines — data flow + key cost/risk annotations). Question header ≤12 chars. Recommended tree is option 1. Skip this step ONLY when user pre-stated their pick OR running non-interactively (e.g. /loop). After user picks: update frontmatter status (pass / working / deferred) and append outcome log. If trees > 4, present top 4 in AskUserQuestion (recommended + 3 highest-value); the file retains all.
expectations: Mentions AskUserQuestion as the default user-pick mechanism
Mentions side-by-side layout (option list left, preview pane right)
Mentions each option has label, description, AND preview fields
Mentions preview is a compact ASCII tree (≤20 lines or similar bound)
Mentions recommended tree gets (Recommended) suffix as option 1
Mentions hybrid recommendations rely on the Other field, not a 5th option
Mentions skip-conditions: pre-stated pick OR non-interactive context
Mentions trees > 4 → top 4 in AskUserQuestion, file retains all

evals.prompt: What does grill-fast log when a user defers the session to backlog without picking a tree?
evals.expected_output: result=partial, pick=none, and the optional deferred_to=<issue-url> field pointing to the backlog issue. Exactly one log row per session — no intermediate row while AskUserQuestion is pending.
expectations: Mentions result=partial for the deferral case
Mentions pick=none for the deferral case
Mentions deferred_to optional field (with issue URL or backlog pointer)
Mentions logging exactly once per session (no intermediate rows)

evals.prompt: The grill-fast topic could legitimately be dropped entirely. What must the AskUserQuestion option set include?
evals.expected_output: An explicit null option — 'don't do this / drop it' — whenever not-doing-it is a live outcome. Never offer only sub-variants: that presupposes the action is settled and makes the question leading. A user in flow clicks the sensible sub-variant, and that click cannot bear the weight of reversing a prior considered decision.
expectations: Mentions an explicit null/drop option when not-doing-it is a live outcome
Mentions offering only sub-variants presupposes the action is settled / is leading
Mentions a user in flow clicking a sub-variant cannot reverse a prior considered decision

evals.prompt: The grill-fast topic references GitHub issues shown in the SessionStart open-tasks list. What must grill-fast do before grilling them?
evals.expected_output: Run gh issue view N on each referenced issue FIRST — confirm it is still open and read its latest comments for prior decisions. The SessionStart open-tasks cache is stale; grilling an already-closed issue contaminates every downstream pick.
expectations: Mentions gh issue view to verify live state before grilling
Mentions the SessionStart open-tasks cache is stale
Mentions reading latest comments for prior decisions
